Account Groups

Account groups are the second level of the chart-of-accounts hierarchy, sitting between account types and individual accounts. Every account belongs to a group, and every group belongs to an account type. Groups are used to organise accounts in reports — for example, grouping all expense accounts into sub-categories like "Operating Expenses" and "Administrative Expenses".

What account groups are

An account group is a named container for a set of accounts under a single account type. For example, under the Asset account type you might have groups called "Current Assets" and "Fixed Assets". Within "Current Assets" you would have individual accounts like "Bank" and "Accounts Receivable". Account groups are used in reports to subtotal related accounts and in the account creation form to determine the normal balance of new accounts.

Account types

Account types are the top-level classifications. They are fixed in the application and cannot be created or deleted through the UI:

Account typeNormal balanceTypical use
AssetDebitThings the business owns (bank, receivables, equipment)
LiabilityCreditThings the business owes (payables, loans)
EquityCreditOwners' interest in the business
Income / RevenueCreditMoney earned from sales or services
ExpenseDebitCosts incurred in running the business

Fields

FieldRequiredNotes
Account typeYesWhich account type this group belongs to
CodeYesShort code for the group, max 20 characters
NameYesDescriptive group name, max 255 characters
DescriptionNoOptional explanation, max 1 000 characters
ActiveYesInactive groups are hidden from selection menus

Managing account groups

  1. Go to Accounting › Account Groups.
  2. Click New Account Group to add a group, or click the edit icon to modify an existing one.
  3. Select the account type, enter a code and name, and save.

The default account groups are seeded on installation. You can add new groups at any time. Accounts already assigned to a group are not affected if the group's name or code is changed.

Permissions

  • account-groups-view-any / account-groups-view — to view account groups
  • account-groups-create — to create new groups
  • account-groups-update — to edit existing groups
  • account-groups-delete — to soft-delete groups
  • account-groups-restore / account-groups-force-delete — to restore or permanently delete groups
